description: Sequencing market: will a US-Iran ceasefire be announced before crude oil (CL) hits $120? Dual-trigger resolution with 50-50 fallback if neither occurs by June 30. Part of Polymarket's 'before' market pattern — a novel market structure that creates implicit conditional probabilities between geopolitical and financial events.
question: US x Iran ceasefire before Oil hits $120?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if US-Iran ceasefire is announced before CL settlement hits $120. Resolves No if CL hits $120 first. Resolves 50-50 if neither occurs by June 30, 2026. Same-day tie broken by ET timestamp.

triggers.description: Official US-Iran ceasefire agreement announced BEFORE crude oil CL active month settlement price reaches $120

trigger_a.definition: Clear public confirmation from both US and Iranian governments of a mutually agreed halt in military hostilities, or confirmed by overwhelming media consensus
trigger_a.excludes: Informal understandings, backchannels, de-escalation without formal agreement, humanitarian pauses, tactical stand-downs, unilateral pauses

trigger_b.definition: Official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of CL futures equals or exceeds $120
trigger_b.excludes: Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, indicative prices — ONLY the official settlement price counts

resolution_logic.trigger_logic: SEQUENCE — first trigger to occur determines resolution
exclusions: Broader peace deals qualify ONLY if they include an explicit, dated commitment to halt fighting
CME settlement price corrections after initial publication are ignored
Only Active Month (front month) settlement counts — not back months

fallback.condition: Neither trigger occurs by June 30, 2026 11:59 PM ET
fallback.resolution: 50-50 (market pays out evenly to both sides)

resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: The ceasefire definition references 'before Trump visits China' in one clause — appears to be a copy-paste artifact from a related market. The oil trigger uses CME settlement, which can differ from last traded price. Active Month rolls ~2 business days before spot month expiration, creating brief windows where the 'front month' changes.

catalyst_dates.event: CME CL settlement — current WTI ~$69, needs 74% rally to trigger No
US-Iran military operations and diplomatic signals
April CL contract expiry — active month roll

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi has no equivalent 'before' sequencing market. They offer daily/weekly WTI price brackets (KXWTI series) and a US-Iran nuclear deal market (KXUSAIRANAGREEMENT), but nothing that links ceasefire timing to oil price. This is a Polymarket-native market structure with no cross-platform comparison possible. Coverage gap.

related_markets.notes: US x Iran ceasefire before Trump visits China — same ceasefire trigger, different second condition
Russia-Ukraine ceasefire before US-Iran ceasefire — ceasefire sequencing between two conflicts
Regime fall would likely precede or accompany ceasefire — 16.5% probability
Ground forces in Iran (65%) would drive oil toward $120 trigger and delay ceasefire
